summary: Package fetches and executes the uv installer at install time
details: run.js (line 75) invokes execSync("curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh") via Node's child_process module. This pipes a remote shell script directly to sh during package execution, with no hash verification, no version pinning, and no opt-out. The astral.sh installer mutates the user's shell rc files to add ~/.local/bin to PATH and writes the uv binary into the user's home directory; whatever bytes the URL serves at the moment of install are executed with the installer's privileges. The fetched runtime (uv, a Python package manager) has no documented relationship to the package's stated purpose, and the script is mutable upstream content rather than a publisher-pinned artifact.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
